final_verdict.summary: PASS. All 14 contract (revision 1) verification commands independently re-run from repo root and all exited 0/PASS -- including the four section-anchored content checks (entries 5-8) that were correctly proven to fail pre-implementation across two rounds of contract review and now correctly pass against the real, authored ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md. This matches the generator's captured evidence: all 14 flat 033-verify-NN.txt files are format-compliant (literal invocation first line, literal EXIT:0 last line, no session-absolute paths) and the consolidated .agent/evidence/033/verification.txt independently re-derived to show all 14 sections passing with a final ALL_PASS=True. The evidence bundle's 0-byte .agent/evidence/033/diff.patch is confirmed legitimate, not stale: feature 033 is a create-only contract (files_modify=[]) whose sole deliverable is a newly-untracked file, which `git diff HEAD` structurally never captures regardless of pathspec; cross-checked against feature 032's own non-empty (6481-byte) diff.patch to confirm the evidence-generation mechanism is sound and that 033's empty patch is a property of its own scope, not a broken capture. The live repo-wide `git diff HEAD --stat` during this QA pass shows exactly the 2 tracked files feature 032 modified and nothing attributable to 033, confirming Mode B's stated attribution. bash tools/validate.sh reports the full default sweep at SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed (exit 0) -- no regression, even with feature 032's own concurrent tracked changes present in the same tree. All 5 of feature 033's acceptance_tests in .agent/feature_list_005.json are satisfied with evidence traceable by path, including independent re-runs of feature_list_005.json's own literal (pre-strengthening) official acceptance-test commands directly against the real file, all of which also pass. Two adversarial mutation probes on disposable scratch copies of the REAL implemented file (not synthetic fixtures) confirm the section-anchored checks for the operator-exception rule and the verdict-artifact path convention are genuinely discriminating against the actual deliverable's content, not accidentally satisfied. No issues, no missing acceptance coverage, no unsupported claims found. Per AGF Sec 5.4 and the precedent this evaluator established in .agent/qa/031.json, feature_list_005.json's F-033 status field and .agent/run_state.json were both deliberately left untouched; this .agent/qa/033.json artifact is the durable pass signal for the orchestrator to act on. No feature-032 surface was modified during this QA pass.
